text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, near a small river surrounded by towering oaks, lived a quaint little owl named Poppy. Poppy had a particular talent for singing old ballads at night. People from all around would gather in their trees to listen.

One evening, Poppy decided to put on a special performance to celebrate finding a rusty old violin under a big oak tree. She strummed chords slowly, each note weaving into a tale of longing hearts and forgotten loves.

As she sang about elusive love reincarnated, a young fox wandered by. His eyes brightened at the music.

"Hey there!" he called. "That's some pretty stuff you're singing!"

Poppy paused and looked at him. "Oh, just my love story," she replied whimsically. "Ever hear of someone who never forgets?"

The fox pondered, "Something like that. I've got this old tale about a girl who spent centuries playing the harp."

Poppy fluttered her
